quotes.text: The best description I can come up with is metallic; a rather pleasant sweet metallic sensation. It reminded me of my college summers where I labored for many hours with an arc welding torch repairing heavy equipment for a small logging outfit. It reminded me of pleasant sweet-smelling welding fumes. That is the smell of space.
quotes.speaker: Don Pettit
quotes.context: Astronaut and ISS Science Officer, describing what space smells like (NASA blog, quoted on NASA.gov)

source.title: December 2021: What does space smell like? - NASA

quotes.text: Where my reason, imagination or interest were not engaged, I would not or I could not learn.
quotes.speaker: Winston Churchill
quotes.context: From Churchill's own autobiography 'My Early Life: A Roving Commission' (1930), in the chapter recalling his schooldays at Harrow where he was placed in the lowest form.

source.title: My Early Life, by Winston S. Churchill (Project Gutenberg Canada full text)

quotes.text: Look, I get asked constantly about the future of the web, and honestly, I think the honest answer is: I don't know, and anyone who tells you they do is selling something.
quotes.speaker: Tim Berners-Lee
quotes.context: SXSW Interactive keynote Q&A session, Austin, Texas, March 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Berners-Lee is known for measured, self-deprecating candor about the unintended consequences of the web he built. The subtle tell is that he more typically frames uncertainty as a call to collective responsibility, not a quip about salesmanship.

quotes.text: Every time I go back to Iceland I remember that the landscape there doesn't care what you think of it. That's the most honest thing I know.
quotes.speaker: Björk
quotes.context: Interview with The Wire magazine, issue 380, October 2015
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Björk constantly frames Icelandic nature as a philosophical reference point and speaks in exactly this kind of oblique, personifying language. The giveaway is almost imperceptible: she'd likely push further into the surreal rather than landing somewhere this cleanly resolved.

quotes.text: The thing about being a villain on screen is you have to find the place inside yourself where you completely agree with them. You can't play a villain if you think he's wrong.
quotes.speaker: Cate Blanchett
quotes.context: SAG-AFTRA Foundation Conversations series, Los Angeles, October 2017
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Blanchett discusses her acting process in exactly this cerebral, non-judgmental vocabulary, often talking about inhabiting characters from the inside out. The giveaway is that she usually credits a specific director or writer as the catalyst for that kind of reflection.

quotes.text: I'm not an animal lover if that means you think things are nice if you can pet them, but I am intoxicated by animals.
quotes.speaker: David Attenborough
quotes.context: Naturalist and broadcaster, in a 2013 Metro interview, on his relationship with wildlife
